pattern: Counting substrings that contain a star is easier as the total minus the star-free runs, and that count only grows with time, so binary search the activation moment.
hints: Substrings with at least one star equal all substrings minus those lying entirely inside a star-free gap.
Each maximal run of unstarred characters of length L hides L*(L+1)/2 substrings.
The valid count never decreases as stars are added, so binary search the earliest time it reaches k.

edge_inputs.description: exactly reaches k at last step

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    import string
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(n))
    order = list(range(n))
    rng.shuffle(order)
    total = n * (n + 1) // 2
    k = rng.randint(1, total + 2)
    return [s, order, k]
